user_query:
I need a thorough code review and cleanup of our pytest tests. Please read all files under input/ (especially input/src/ and input/tests/ plus input/conftest.py), identify issues in async testing, fixtures, parametrization, and mocking, and then produce corrected tests and a concise review report.

Deliverables:
1) Write a structured review to output/review.md that lists the issues you found (group by async testing, fixtures, parametrization, and mocking) and briefly explains the specific fixes you applied.
2) Write corrected test files to:
   - output/tests/test_user_service.py (fix async patterns using @pytest.mark.asyncio, use AsyncMock for async dependencies, await all coroutines, verify mock call arguments, remove over-mocking of private helpers, and replace the duplicated email tests with a single @pytest.mark.parametrize test that uses pytest.param with descriptive id strings)
   - output/tests/test_patching.py (fix the patch target so it patches where the function is used, not where it is defined; prefer using the pytest-mock mocker fixture for automatic cleanup)
   - output/tests/conftest.py (ensure any async fixtures use yield with try/finally for cleanup, choose sensible fixture scope, and remove unnecessary autouse usage)

Notes and constraints:
- Do not change any files under input/src/; only modify tests and fixture files in your outputs.
- Ensure there are no Mock objects used for async call sites in your corrected tests; use AsyncMock instead.
- Ensure at least one parametrized test uses pytest.param with explicit id values for readability.
- Ensure that any patch of external_api_call targets the module where it is imported and used (module_b), not where it is defined (module_a), and that you verify behavior.
- Keep paths in outputs exactly as specified.
